TO-Master: an LLM-agent framework for automated topology optimization
description: Topology optimization (TO) has become a mature computational design method, but using it still requires substantial manual effort in geometry preparation, mesh generation, boundary-condition assignment, solver setup, and postprocessing. This implementation barrier limits the use of TO outside expert workflows, even when differentiable finite element solvers are available. This work introduces TO-M
authors: Haoju Lin, Wenchang Zhang, Weipeng Xu

title: ContextNest: Verifiable Context Governance for Autonomous AI Agent
description: Autonomous AI agents increasingly depend on external knowledge stores, yet most retrieval pipelines provide relevance without durable guarantees of provenance, version identity, integrity, traceability, or point-in-time reconstruction. We formalize this as context governance and present ContextNext, an open specification and reference implementation for governed AI-consumable knowledge vaults. Con
authors: Misha Sulpovar, Benn R. Konsynski, Qaish Kanchwala

title: UA-ChatDev: Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Agent Collaboration for Reliable Software Development
description: Software development is a complex task that demands cooperation among agents with diverse roles. Large language models (LLMs) have enabled autonomous multi-agent software development frameworks that leverage role-based collaboration to automate requirements analysis, coding, testing, and refinement. However, existing approaches typically assume that intermediate agent outputs are equally reliable,
authors: Temitayo Olamilekan Ogunsusi, Lijun Qian, Xishuang Dong
